title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.imageAlt: A beautifully presented whole slow roasted lamb ouzi with rice, nuts, and herbs, ready for a Middle Eastern feast.
seo.imageCaption: Impress your guests with this stunning and flavorful authentic Lamb Ouzi, slow-roasted to tender perfection.

description: Lamb Ouzi is a majestic Middle Eastern dish featuring a whole slow-roasted lamb, often served atop fragrant spiced rice. This recipe guides you to achieve incredibly tender, fall-off-the-bone lamb bursting with traditional flavors. It is a true centerpiece for any celebratory gathering, promising an unforgettable culinary experience.

ingredients.notes: mix of cinnamon, cardamom, nutmeg, black pepper, allspice

instructions.title: Prepare the Lamb Marinade
instructions.text: In a bowl, combine the Ouzi spice blend, minced garlic, olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper. Mix well to form a thick paste. Score the lamb shoulder or leg deeply all over with a sharp knife to allow the marinade to penetrate. Rub the spice paste generously all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into the scores. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight, for best flavor.

instructions.title: Slow Roast the Lamb
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 160°C (325°F). Place the marinated lamb in a large roasting pan. Add about 1 cup of water or broth to the bottom of the pan to prevent drying and create steam. Cover the roasting pan tightly with foil. Roast for 3.5 to 4 hours, or until the lamb is incredibly tender and easily pulls away from the bone. For the last 30 minutes, remove the foil to allow the lamb to brown and crisp slightly.

instructions.title: Prepare the Ouzi Rice Base
instructions.text: While the lamb is roasting, drain the soaked basmati rice. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat vegetable oil or ghee over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and saute until softened, about 5-7 minutes. If using, add the ground lamb or beef and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon. Drain any excess fat.

instructions.title: Cook the Spiced Rice
instructions.text: Add the drained basmati rice, frozen peas, and diced carrots to the pot. Stir in 1 tablespoon of the Ouzi spice blend and a pinch of salt. Pour in the chicken or lamb broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender. Do not lift the lid during cooking. Once cooked, let it rest for 5 minutes off the heat, then fluff with a fork.

instructions.title: Assemble the Lamb Ouzi
instructions.text: Once the lamb is roasted and rested for 15 minutes, carefully transfer it to a large serving platter. The meat should be very tender. Spread the prepared Ouzi rice generously around the base of the platter. You can gently shred some of the lamb and mix it with the rice, or serve the whole piece of lamb on top.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Garnish the entire dish with a generous sprinkle of toasted pine nuts, toasted almond slivers, and freshly chopped parsley. Serve the Lamb Ouzi immediately as a grand centerpiece for your Middle Eastern feast. It pairs wonderfully with a side of yogurt cucumber salad or a simple green salad.

tips: For extra moisture and flavor, add a few lemon slices and bay leaves to the roasting pan under the lamb.
If you prefer a richer rice, you can toast the rice briefly in the ghee before adding the broth, enhancing its nutty flavor.
Leftover Lamb Ouzi can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat gently, adding a splash of broth to prevent drying.
article:
Lamb Ouzi: The Ultimate Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern Feast

Imagine a dish so grand, so rich in flavor and history, that it becomes the centerpiece of every celebration, every family gathering, every moment that calls for something truly special. That dish is Lamb Ouzi (also spelled Qoozi or Ghozi), a magnificent culinary masterpiece from the heart of the Middle East. More than just a meal, it's an experience – a testament to the art of slow cooking, where a whole lamb (or a significant portion) is roasted to fall-off-the-bone perfection, often stuffed with aromatic rice, nuts, and spices. This article will guide you through the journey of preparing this epic feast, ensuring your Lamb Ouzi is nothing short of legendary.

## What is Lamb Ouzi?

Lamb Ouzi is a quintessential Middle Eastern dish, revered for its impressive presentation and unparalleled taste. At its core, it involves a whole lamb, or a large cut like a leg or shoulder, slow-roasted until incredibly tender. The magic doesn't stop there; the lamb is typically served atop a bed of fragrant, spiced rice, often studded with toasted nuts like almonds, pistachios, and pine nuts, and sometimes dried fruits like raisins. Each region might have its subtle variations, but the essence remains: a deeply flavorful, aromatic, and incredibly moist lamb, infused with spices and paired with equally rich rice. It’s a dish reserved for grand occasions – weddings, Eid celebrations, or large family gatherings – symbolizing hospitality and abundance. The slow roasting process is key, breaking down the tough fibers of the lamb to create a melt-in-your-mouth texture that is simply unforgettable.

## Ingredients for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi

Crafting the perfect Lamb Ouzi requires a selection of fresh, high-quality ingredients. While a whole lamb is traditional, a large lamb shoulder or leg can be used for a more manageable feast.

For the Lamb:
*   1 whole lamb (approx. 20-30 lbs / 9-14 kg) or 1 large lamb leg/shoulder (approx. 5-7 lbs / 2.3-3.2 kg)
*   1/2 cup olive oil
*   1/4 cup lemon juice
*   1/4 cup apple cider vinegar
*   1 head garlic, minced
*   2 tbsp Baharat (Middle Eastern 7-spice blend)
*   1 tbsp ground cinnamon
*   1 tbsp ground cardamom
*   1 tbsp ground cumin
*   1 tbsp smoked paprika
*   2 tsp salt (or to taste)
*   1 tsp black pepper
*   Optional: 1-2 onions, quartered, for the roasting pan

For the Aromatic Rice Stuffing:
*   3 cups Basmati rice, soaked for 30 minutes and drained
*   1/2 cup ghee or butter
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   500g ground lamb or beef (optional, for a richer stuffing)
*   1/2 cup toasted almonds, slivered or whole
*   1/2 cup toasted pine nuts
*   1/2 cup toasted pistachios (shelled)
*   1/4 cup raisins or dried cranberries (optional)
*   2 tsp Baharat
*   1 tsp ground cinnamon
*   1/2 tsp ground cardamom
*   1/2 tsp ground nutmeg
*   Salt and black pepper to taste
*   5-6 cups hot lamb or chicken broth

## How to Make Lamb Ouzi (Step-by-Step)

Preparing this exquisite Lamb Ouzi Recipe is a labor of love, but the results are profoundly rewarding.

1.  **Prepare the Lamb:** Pat the lamb dry thoroughly. In a bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, vinegar, minced garlic, Baharat, cinnamon, cardamom, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper. Rub this marinade generously all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into all crevices. If using a whole lamb, make small incisions to allow the marinade to penetrate deeper. Cover and refrigerate for at least 12 hours, preferably 24 hours.
2.  **Prepare the Rice Stuffing:** Heat ghee in a large pot. Sauté the chopped onion until translucent. Add the optional ground lamb/beef and cook until browned, breaking it up. Drain any excess fat. Add the soaked and drained Basmati rice, Baharat, cinnamon, cardamom, nutmeg, salt, and pepper. Stir well to coat the rice.
3.  **Cook the Rice:** Pour in the hot broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for about 15-20 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is partially cooked (it will finish cooking inside the lamb). Stir in the toasted nuts and raisins (if using).
4.  **Stuff and Truss (if applicable):** Preheat oven to 300°F (150°C). If using a whole lamb, carefully stuff the cavity with the prepared rice. Sew or skewer the opening shut to seal in the stuffing. If using a leg or shoulder, you can place the lamb on a bed of rice in a large roasting pan or serve the rice alongside.
5.  **Roast the Lamb:** Place the stuffed lamb in a large roasting pan. You can add quartered onions or a little water to the bottom of the pan to keep it moist. Cover the entire pan tightly with foil or a roasting bag. Slow roast for 4-6 hours for a large leg/shoulder, or 8-12 hours for a whole lamb, or until the internal temperature reaches 190-200°F (88-93°C) and the meat is incredibly tender. For the last 30-45 minutes, remove the foil to allow the skin to crisp up.
6.  **Rest and Serve:** Once roasted, remove the lamb from the oven and let it rest, loosely tented with foil, for at least 30 minutes before carving. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a moist and tender result.

## Tips for Perfectly Tender Lamb Ouzi

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the lamb sits in the spice mixture, the more flavor it will absorb, and the more tender it will become.
*   **Low and Slow:** Patience is a virtue when it comes to Lamb Ouzi. The low oven temperature and extended cooking time are crucial for breaking down the tough connective tissues, resulting in melt-in-your-mouth tenderness.
*   **Keep it Moist:** Covering the lamb tightly with foil or using a roasting bag traps steam, preventing the lamb from drying out during its long roast. You can also add a cup of water or broth to the bottom of the pan.
*   **Rest the Meat:** This step is non-negotiable. Resting allows the muscle fibers to relax and reabsorb the juices, ensuring a succulent and flavorful lamb.
*   **Quality Lamb:** Source good quality lamb. Grass-fed lamb often has better flavor.

## Serving Suggestions for Lamb Ouzi

Lamb Ouzi is a feast in itself, but it shines even brighter with traditional accompaniments.
*   **Yogurt Sauce:** A simple, refreshing yogurt sauce (plain yogurt with minced garlic and dried mint) offers a delightful contrast to the rich lamb.
*   **Fresh Salads:** Crisp, tangy salads like Fattoush (with toasted pita bread, vegetables, and sumac dressing) or Tabbouleh (parsley and bulgur salad) cut through the richness.
*   **Pickles:** Assorted Middle Eastern pickles (torshi) add a zesty, briny counterpoint.
*   **Garnish:** A sprinkle of fresh parsley or mint, and extra toasted nuts, adds color and texture.
*   **Presentation:** For a truly grand presentation, serve the whole roasted lamb on a large platter, surrounded by the aromatic rice, and garnished beautifully.

## Lamb Ouzi Variations & Substitutions

While the traditional Lamb Ouzi Recipe is magnificent, you can adapt it to your needs:
*   **Lamb Cut:** If a whole lamb is too daunting, a large bone-in lamb leg or shoulder works beautifully. You can debone it partially to create a pocket for stuffing.
*   **Rice Base:** While Basmati is classic, you can experiment with other long-grain rice or even freekeh for a smoky flavor.
*   **Nut Mix:** Feel free to use your favorite blend of toasted nuts. Walnuts or cashews can be added for variety.
*   **Spice Adjustments:** Adjust the spices to your preference. Add a pinch of dried rose petals or a little saffron to the rice for an extra layer of aroma.
*   **Vegetarian Stuffing:** For guests who prefer a lighter option, you can prepare some plain spiced rice without meat and serve it alongside.

## Frequently Asked Questions About Lamb Ouzi

**Q: Can I prepare Lamb Ouzi ahead of time?**
A: You can marinate the lamb up to 24 hours in advance. The rice stuffing can be prepared a few hours ahead. The roasted lamb is best served fresh, but leftovers are delicious and can be gently reheated.

**Q: What if I don't have a very large oven for a whole lamb?**
A: A large lamb leg or shoulder is an excellent alternative and fits in most standard ovens. You can still create a stunning, flavorful dish.

**Q: Is Lamb Ouzi spicy?**
A: It's typically aromatic and flavorful rather than spicy. The spices used (Baharat, cinnamon, cardamom) provide warmth and depth, not heat. You can adjust the amount of black pepper to your preference.

**Q: How long does the entire process take?**
A: The active preparation time is relatively moderate (1-2 hours), but the marination (12-24 hours) and slow roasting (4-12 hours depending on the lamb size) mean it requires significant planning. It's a perfect weekend project or a dish for special occasions.

Embarking on the journey of making Lamb Ouzi is an unforgettable culinary adventure. This Lamb Ouzi Recipe is more than just a meal; it's a celebration of heritage, flavor, and the joy of sharing. The aroma filling your kitchen, the tender meat falling off the bone, and the beautifully spiced rice will transport you to the heart of Middle Eastern hospitality. Prepare to impress your guests and create lasting memories with this magnificent feast.
